Teaching 1980s AI to Trade Forex: Inside My MQL5 Bot That Combines TradingView Signals, Hopfield Networks, and a Boltzmann Machine

12 min read
1 hour ago

--

What happens when you make a trading algorithm double-check itself using two neural network architectures older than most traders alive today
Press enter or click to view image in full size

There’s a certain irony in using 1982-era physics-inspired neural networks and 1980s-era Boltzmann Machines to trade 2026 forex markets. Hopfield networks and Restricted Boltzmann Machines (RBMs) were largely superseded decades ago by transformers, LSTMs, and diffusion models. Nobody trains a Hopfield network to do anything serious anymore.
That’s exactly why I wanted to use them.
Modern deep learning models are black boxes trained on backpropagation and gradient descent over millions of parameters. Hopfield networks and RBMs are small, interpretable, and — crucially for a retail algo trader without a GPU cluster — cheap to train inside a MetaTrader 5 terminal, in native MQL5, on a single CPU core, in under a second per symbol.
This article walks through an Expert Advisor (EA) I built that:

• Replicates the logic behind TradingView’s “Technical Summary” indicator (Buy / Sell / Strong Buy / Strong…
